title: Acids, Bases, Salts and the pH Scale
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Define pH as $-\log_{10}[H^+]$ on a scale from 0 to 14.
Simulate Universal Indicator color transitions and neutralisation reactions.

objectives.text: Slide the pH slider to 2.4 (Lemon Juice) and observe RED color.
Slide the pH slider to 11.5 (Ammonia) and observe PURPLE color.

theory: Acids release $H^+$ ions in aqueous solutions (pH < 7). Bases release $OH^-$ ions (pH > 7). Universal indicator displays a rainbow spectrum according to $[H^+]$ concentration.
hints: Drag the pH spectrum slider from 0 to 14 to see the beaker liquid change color.
Check the Substance indicator card to see household examples.

tasks.instruction: Move the pH slider away from neutral pH 7.0.

observationPrompts: How does hydrogen ion concentration $[H^+]$ change as pH decreases from 7 to 2?

realWorldExamples.title: Soil pH in Agriculture
realWorldExamples.description: Farmers adjust soil pH using lime (base) or sulfur (acid) to optimize crop yield.

commonMistakes: Thinking a lower pH value means lower acidity; lower pH actually means HIGHER $[H^+]$ concentration.
